text: fat is 49.2g. omega3 is where things start to fall off. for nuts, fat quality has to justify the calorie density.
carbs is 16.1g. the fibre support is doing a lot of the work. for a nut, good fats have to justify the calorie density.
protein is 25.8g. protein quality is a real strength here. protein is support here, not the whole argument.
vitamin e at 56% DV, vitamin b12 at 0% DV stand out most. that is only mild support overall.
magnesium at 42% DV, zinc at 28% DV, iron at 25% DV stand out most. useful, but still not a major mineral edge.

on the plus side. unusually protein-forward for a nut because tests whether nuts can handle an unusually high-protein member. cheap and practical because real-world advantage. strong satiety because useful practical upside.

the drawbacks next. allergy risk matters because meaningful downside. omega-3 support is weak because a big test of the current nuts philosophy. awkward category identity because botanically a legume, culturally a nut.
B tier. Best in small amounts where the fats and minerals matter more than sheer calories.
